info.title: Ledger Service
info.description:
**Ledger Service API v3.73.6**

## Overview
The Ledger Service provides a comprehensive REST API for managing the core ledger structure including entities, ledgers, accounts, and chart of accounts. It serves as the foundational layer for multi-tenant financial accounting systems.

## Authentication
All API requests require authentication via API key:
- **Header**: `x-api-key`
- **Format**: `x-api-key: <your-api-key>`
- **Example**: `x-api-key: your-api-key-here`

Note: The header name must be lowercase.

## Key Features
- **Multi-Tenancy**: Isolated sites for different platform instances and environments
- **Entity Management**: Companies, individuals, and custodian entities with full lifecycle management
- **Ledger Management**: Entity-currency ledgers that organize accounts by ownership and currency
- **Account Management**: Financial accounts with support for:
  - Linked account relationships (nostro/vostro correspondent accounts, intercompany pairs)
  - Dimensional ownership model (entity scope, counterparty type, functional domain, valuation role)
  - Account hierarchies and rollup structures
- **Chart of Accounts**: Flexible account code definitions with dimensional attributes for sophisticated classification
- **Advanced Filtering & Search**: Every listing endpoint supports rich filtering (including equals/not equals/in/not-in operators) and case-insensitive search fields. Account searches span account name, external identifier, and UUID; entity searches cover entity name and external ID; ledger searches include ledger name and description.
- **Reference Data**: Currencies and countries with multi-tenancy support

## Architecture
The service implements a dimensional ownership model where accounts are classified by:
- **Entity Scope**: HOUSE (company-owned) | CLIENT (customer-owned) | GROUP (intercompany)
- **Counterparty Type**: CLIENT | INTERCOMPANY | SCHEME | STATUTORY | EXTERNAL
- **Functional Domain**: OPERATIONAL | SETTLEMENT | TAX | CONTINGENT
- **Valuation Role**: PRIMARY | CONTRA | OFF_BALANCE_SHEET

This API enables building compliant multi-entity, multi-currency accounting systems with proper segregation and linked account relationships.

get.summary: Check the health of the application
get.description: Health check endpoint used by ALB target groups. Returns 503 during startup (cache preload) and 200 once the service is ready to accept traffic. The response body uses the platform HealthResponse contract (shared with event-service and balance-service), not RFC 9457 ProblemDetail.

FsliGroup.title: FsliGroup
FsliGroup.description:
IFRS financial statement groupings per IAS 1 and IAS 7.

These groupings provide a hierarchical structure for financial reporting,
allowing aggregation of individual FSLI line items into standard categories.

enum: Accumulated depreciation on property plant and equipment
Cash held on behalf of clients
Loss allowance on loans and advances
Investments in subsidiaries associates and joint ventures
Net fair value gains/losses on financial instruments
Financial assets at fair value through profit or loss
Financial assets at fair value through other comprehensive income
Financial liabilities at fair value through profit or loss

IFRS Financial Statement Line Item enumeration.

FunctionalDomain.description:

FunctionalType.title: FunctionalType
FunctionalType.description:
Functional classification of account codes for reconciliation.

Classifies account codes by their role in reconciliation and operational workflows.

InstitutionType.title: InstitutionType
InstitutionType.description:
Business classification for entities that own ledgers.

Used for financial reporting and regulatory categorization. Only applicable
to COMPANY entities that own ledgers; INDIVIDUAL entities should have null.

ValuationRole.title: ValuationRole

Financial statement treatment.

Defines how the account is treated in financial statements.

ValidationErrorDetail.description:
Structured validation error for a specific field.

Used in the `errors` extension member to provide field-level
validation failure details.

field.description: The field path that failed validation (e.g., 'name' or 'records[0].ledger_id')
field.title: Field

message.description: Human-readable description of the validation failure
examples: Value must be a positive integer
message.title: Message

code.description: Machine-readable error code for programmatic handling
code.title: Code

ValidationErrorDetail.title: ValidationErrorDetail

ProblemDetail.description:
RFC 9457 Problem Details response model.

This model represents the standard error response format for HTTP APIs.
All error responses from ledger-service should use this format with
content-type: application/problem+json

Attributes:
    type: A URI reference identifying the problem type. When set to
        "about:blank", the title field should be used to identify the
        problem type.
    title: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type. Should
        be the same for all occurrences of this problem type.
    status: The HTTP status code generated by the origin server.
    detail: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence
        of the problem.
    instance: A URI reference identifying the specific occurrence of
        the problem. Typically the request path.
    errors: Optional list of field-level validation errors. This is an
        extension member per RFC 9457 Section 3.2.

examples.detail: Account not found in ledger config service.
examples.title: Not Found

type.description: A URI reference that identifies the problem type. Use 'about:blank' when the title alone is sufficient.
type.title: Type

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type
title.title: Title

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence
examples: Account not found in ledger config service.
detail.title: Detail

instance.description: A URI reference identifying the specific occurrence (typically the request path)
instance.title: Instance

errors.description: Optional list of field-level validation errors
errors.title: Errors

ProblemDetail.title: ProblemDetail
